name: Laad Bazaar Bangle Shopping
description: Laad Bazaar is a 400-year-old market street located adjacent to Charminar, famous throughout India for its lac bangles, bridal jewellery, and traditional Hyderabadi handicrafts. The word Laad means lac, the resin used to make the distinctive bangles that are decorated with mirror work, stones, and gold paint. The market stretches for nearly a kilometre through narrow lanes packed with hundreds of shops. It is especially vibrant during wedding season and Eid. Beyond bangles, the market sells pearl jewellery, attar (traditional perfumes), embroidered fabric, and Hyderabadi footwear called Kolhapuri chappals.

name: Falaknuma Palace Tour
description: Falaknuma Palace, meaning Mirror of the Sky, is a 32-acre palace built in 1884 by Nawab Vikar-ul-Umra and later acquired by the sixth Nizam. It was converted into a luxury hotel by the Taj group and opened to guests in 2010 after a decade of meticulous restoration. The palace is built in the shape of a scorpion with Palladian and Tudor architectural styles, featuring the world's largest private collection of Venetian chandeliers, a 101-seater dining table, Bohemian crystal, Meissen porcelain, and Murano glass. Guided heritage tours are available for non-resident visitors. The horse carriage ride from the gate to the palace is iconic.
best_time: Morning 10am-12pm, advance booking required for tours

name: Hyderabad Pearls at Mangatrai
description: Hyderabad is known as the City of Pearls and has been a global centre of the pearl trade for over 400 years. Mangatrai Pearls in Secunderabad is one of the most reputed pearl jewellery establishments in India, offering certified natural, cultured, and freshwater pearls. The store conducts guided demonstrations of pearl grading and processing. The Hyderabadi pearl set — a traditional choker necklace — is one of the most prized souvenirs from the city. Other reputed pearl dealers are located in Laad Bazaar and Patthargatti. Understanding the pearl grading system is essential before purchasing.

name: Shilparamam Arts and Crafts Village
description: Shilparamam is a living crafts village and cultural centre spread over 65 acres in Hitech City, designed to preserve and promote traditional arts and crafts of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. The village features over 300 permanent craft stalls run by artisans from across the state selling Kalamkari textiles, Nirmal paintings, Kondapalli wooden toys, Etikoppaka lacquerware, and Bidriware metalwork. The complex hosts weekly cultural programs, folk dance performances, and craft demonstrations. It is the best place in Hyderabad to buy authentic handmade crafts directly from artisans at fair prices.

name: Nizam's Museum at Purani Haveli
description: The Nizam's Museum is housed inside the historic Purani Haveli palace in the old city, once the residence of the sixth Nizam, Mir Mahbub Ali Khan. The museum's centrepiece is an extraordinary wardrobe that is recorded in the Guinness Book of World Records as the world's largest wardrobe, measuring 74 metres in length and containing over 3000 garments of the Nizam. The collection includes vintage Rolls-Royce cars, palanquins, royal gifts from foreign dignitaries, ceremonial weapons, and personal effects of the Nizams. The architecture of Purani Haveli itself is worth the visit.

name: Ravindra Bharathi Cultural Centre
description: Ravindra Bharathi is the premier performing arts venue in Hyderabad, built in 1961 and named after the poet Rabindranath Tagore. The auditorium hosts regular performances of classical Carnatic and Hindustani music, Kuchipudi and Bharatanatyam dance recitals, Telugu theatre productions, and annual cultural festivals. The venue is the heartbeat of Hyderabad's classical arts scene. Attending an evening performance here is a deeply authentic cultural experience. The schedule of events is published monthly on the Telangana government arts portal.
best_time: Evening performances typically start at 6pm or 7pm

name: Badshahi Ashurkhana
description: Badshahi Ashurkhana is a 16th century Shia Muslim shrine built in 1594 by Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah, making it one of the oldest buildings in Hyderabad. It is used as a congregation hall for Shia Muslims during the month of Muharram for the Ashura mourning ceremonies. The interior features exquisite tile work with intricate blue and white Persian floral patterns that cover the walls and pillars. The alam (standards) collection inside the shrine are considered sacred. The building is an outstanding example of Persian decorative arts in India and is largely overlooked by mainstream tourism.

name: Telangana State Archaeology Museum
description: The Telangana State Archaeology Museum, located in the Public Gardens, is one of the oldest museums in India, established in 1930. It houses an extensive collection of archaeological finds from across the Deccan plateau including prehistoric tools, Buddhist sculptures from Nagarjunakonda, Kakatiya bronzes, copper plates, inscriptions, and numismatic collections. The Egyptian mummy displayed here is one of only a few genuine mummies in Indian museums. The museum building itself is a fine example of Indo-Saracenic architecture. Entry is free and the museum is often uncrowded, making it a peaceful heritage experience.
